paper_title: Clieben and Brockwitz Study Sites Analysis

methods.description: Micromorphological analysis of soil samples to identify pedogenic transformations and anthropogenic activities.

steps.description: Micromorphological analyses of samples taken from profile 3 prove that the upper overbank fines contain disorthic (reworked) iron oxide nodules and charcoal pieces.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze soil samples for micromorphological features

steps.description: Pedogenic transformation of the Holocene overbank fines is rather weak, as only few small clay infillings and coatings of voids could be detected.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pedogenic transformations in soil samples

methods.description: Analysis of geochemical ratios to assess weathering intensity and soil formation processes.

steps.description: The small VWI ratios in the upper, clay-rich C horizon show obvious preweathering of the substrate.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate VWI ratios to assess preweathering

steps.description: The commonly used Rb/Sr ratio is based on different ion radii, since the much smaller Sr2+ ion is more easily leached during weathering, whereas Rb is fixed to clay.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Rb/Sr ratios to assess weathering

steps.description: Increasing K2O/Na2O ratios indicate chemical weathering.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate K2O/Na2O ratios to assess chemical weathering
